# Microsoft Teams Connector - Full Documentation

# Microsoft Teams Connector 1.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Microsoft Teams (Microsoft Teams Connector) gives you access to the Microsoft
Teams APIs. This connector exposes the most commonly used operations provided by the Microsoft Teams
APIs.

For compatibility information and fixed issues, see the Microsoft Teams Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Microsoft Teams APIs
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the target resource
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

- Send notifications

  Users are notified in Microsoft Teams when a new resource is created or modified in another
  service, for example, when a new Salesforce case is created.

- Onboard employees

  When a group of people are hired at the same time, and human resources has an email in Gmail or a
  spreadsheet in Google Sheets that contains information about the new hires, HR can use the email
  or spreadsheet to trigger different actions:
  - Add users to a workspace
  - Create a channel for all new hires and use it to instantly share important onboarding
    information with them
  - Archive the new hire channel after onboarding is completed

    After the onboarding process is completed, resources can be freed or reused for the next
    onboarding process.

- Create new teams for business projects

  Create teams for new business projects to streamline and automate the management of team members,
  for example:
  - After a new team is created for a new business project, the client can quickly and automatically
    integrate this new team by using Microsoft Teams.
  - After teams are created, as each new employee is hired, the client can automatically execute a
    series of actions to integrate the new hire by using Microsoft Teams.
  - When a team member is either removed or moved to another team, the client can execute a series
    of automated steps to reflect this change within Microsoft Teams.
  - When the business project ends, the resources allocated for that business project in Microsoft
    Teams can be reused or removed.
  - If a manager is hired or replaced, the corresponding group owner of the team can be changed.

## Authentication Types

Microsoft Teams Connector connections use the following OAuth 2.0 grant types:

- OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant type, which uses an access token from the Microsoft identity
  platform for authentication
- OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant type, which uses the identity of the application to access
  web-hosted resources

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Microsoft Teams Reference](microsoft-teams-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Microsoft Teams Connector 1.1 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

These examples show you how to use Microsoft Teams Connector to perform the following actions:

- [Create a new team](#create-new-team)
- [Retrieve team properties and relationships](#retrieve-team)
- [Retrieve a list of team members](#retrieve-members)
- [Retrieve a list of team channels](#retrieve-channel-list)
- [Onboard a new hire to the team](#onboard-new-hire)
- [Delete a team channel](#delete-channel)
- [Remove a team member](#remove-team-member)

## Configure OAuth Authorization

Because this connector uses OAuth to secure connections, you must specify OAuth field values, such
as client credentials, keys, and OAuth-related URLs. To do this:

- Create a configuration file for the OAuth connection
- Configure the OAuth-related global elements
- Configure a global element for the properties file

### Create a Configuration File for the OAuth Connection

Create a configuration file for the OAuth connection that includes properties for the
`Authorization Code` and `Client Credentials` grant types:

1.  Create a file named `mule-app.properties` in the `/src/main/resources/` folder.
2.  In the `mule-app.properties` file, create a set of properties for the `Authorization Code` grant
    type, similar to the ones that follow, replacing the bracketed text (including the brackets)
    with the correct values for your OAuth configuration:

    config.oauth.authorization.code.consumerKey=<consumerKey>
    config.oauth.authorization.code.consumerSecret=<consumerSecret>
    config.oauth.authorization.code.authorizationUrl=<authorizationUrl>
    config.oauth.authorization.code.accessTokenUrl=<accessTokenUrl>

3.  In the `mule-app.properties` file, create a set of properties for the `Client Credentials` grant
    type, similar to the one that follows, replacing the bracketed text (including the brackets)
    with the correct values for your OAuth configuration:

    config.oauth.client.credentials.clientId=<clientId>
    config.oauth.client.credentials.clientSecret=<clientSecret>
    config.oauth.client.credentials.tokenUrl=<tokenUrl>

For more information about creating a properties file, see
[Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

### Configure the OAuth Global Elements

Configure global elements for the `OAuth_Authorization_Code_Config` and
`OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config` files:

1.  Create a new Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange** and enter `microsoft teams`.
3.  Add **Microsoft Teams Connector** to the **Selected modules** section and click **Finish**.
4.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
5.  Select **Connector Configuration > Microsoft Teams Authorization Code Config** and click **OK**.
6.  Enter the following values:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p><code>OAuth_Authorization_Code_Config</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer key</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.authorization.code.consumerKey}</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer secret</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.authorization.code.consumerSecret}</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization url</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.authorization.code.authorizationUrl}</code></p></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access token url</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.authorization.code.accessTokenUrl}</code></p></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><p>Leave the default: <code>https://graph.microsoft.com/.default</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener config</p></td><td><p><code>auth</code></p></td><td><p>Configuration for the HTTP Listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback path</p></td><td><p><code>/callback</code></p></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize path</p></td><td><p><code>authorizePath</code></p></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External callback url</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8083/callback</code></p></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

7.  Click **OK**.
8.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
9.  Select **Connector Configuration > Microsoft Teams Client Credentials Config** and click **OK**.
10. Enter the following values:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p><code>Microsoft_Teams_Client_Credentials_Config</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client id</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.client.credentials.clientId}</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth client ID, as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client secret</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.client.credentials.clientSecret}</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth client secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token url</p></td><td><p><code>${config.oauth.client.credentials.tokenUrl}</code></p></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs token endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><p>Leave the default: <code>https://graph.microsoft.com/.default</code></p></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

11. Click **OK**.

### Configure a Global Element for the Properties File

Configure a global element for the `mule-app.properties` file so that Mule knows where to find it:

1.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
2.  In the **Choose Global Type** dialog, select **Configuration properties** and click **OK**.
3.  In the **File** field, enter `mule.app.properties`.
4.  Click **OK**.

## Create a New Team

The following screenshot shows the Studio app flow for creating a new team:

![Create a new team flow](_images/ms-teams-create-team.png)

Figure 1. Use a flow like this one to create a new team.

Creating a new team involves configuring an HTTP **Listener** component, a **Transform Message**
component, a **Create Team** operation, and a second **Transform Message** component.

To create the flow:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, search for **HTTP** and select the **Listener** operation.
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Accept the defaults.
6.  In the HTTP properties window, set the **Path** field to `/createTeam`.

### Add the First Transform Message Component

The first **Transform Message** component creates a template for the input used to create the team:

1.  In the Mule Palette view, search for **transform message**.
2.  Drag the **Transform Message** component onto the canvas, to the right of the **Listener**
    component.
3.  In the **Transform Message** configuration, overlay the brackets in the **Output** section with
    this XML:

    ```dataweave
    {
    	"template@odata.bind": "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teamsTemplates('standard')",
    	description: message.attributes.queryParams.description,
    	displayName: message.attributes.queryParams.displayName,
    	"members":[
          {
            "@odata.type":"#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember",
            "user@odata.bind":"https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users('" ++ message.attributes.queryParams.user as String ++ "')",
             "roles":[
                "owner"
             ]
          }
    }
    ```

### Add the Create Team Operation

The **Create team** operation creates a new team based on user input:

1.  Drag the **Create team** operation onto the canvas, to the right of the **Transform Message**
    component.
2.  In the Create team configuration, click the **Connector configuration** dropdown and select
    **Microsoft-Teams-Client-Config**.
3.  Select **Microsoft_Teams_Client_Credentials_Config** as the global element type and click
    **OK**.
4.  Configure the following fields in the Create team properties window:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display Name</p></td><td><p><code>Create team</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connector Configuration</p></td><td><p><code>OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><p><code>payload</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Add the Second Transform Message Component

This **Transform Message** component converts the output of the **Create team** operation to JSON
format.

1.  In the Mule **Palette** view, search for `transform message`:
2.  Drag the **Transform Message** component onto the canvas, to the right of the **Listener**
    component.
3.  Click the **Transform Message** component and set the output to `application/json`:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ```

## Retrieve the Team Properties and Relationships

Create a second flow to retrieve the properties and relationships for the new team. Use the **Get
Team** operation in this flow:

![Retrieve the team flow](_images/ms-teams-get-team.png)

Figure 2. Use a flow like this one to retrieve the new teamâs properties and relationships.

## Retrieve the Team Members

Create a third flow to retrieve information about the members of the new team. Use the **List team
members** operation in this flow:

![Retrieve the team members flow](_images/ms-teams-get-members.png)

Figure 3. Use a flow like this one to retrieve information about team members.

## Retrieve the Team Channels

Create a fourth flow to retrieve information about the channels used by the team. Use the **List
channels** operation in this flow:

![Retrieve the team channels flow](_images/ms-teams-list-channels.png)

Figure 4. Use a flow like this one to retrieve the channels used by the new team.

## Onboard a New Hire to the Team

Create a fifth flow to onboard a new hire to the team. Use the following operations in this flow:

- **Add team member** to add a new member to the team
- **Create channel** to create a new channel
- **Add channel member** to add the new member to the newly created channel
- **Create message** to create the welcome message

![Onboarding a new hire flow](_images/ms-teams-onboarding.png)

Figure 5. Use a flow like this one to onboard a new user.

## Delete a Team Channel

Create a sixth flow to delete a channel. Use the **Delete channel** operation in this flow.

![Delete a channel flow](_images/ms-teams-delete-channel.png)

Figure 6. Use a flow like this one to delete a channel.

## Remove a Team Member

Create a seventh flow to remove a member from a team. Use the **Remove team member** operation in
this flow.

![Remove a team member flow](_images/ms-teams-remove-member.png)

Figure 7. Use a flow like this one to remove a team member.

## Run the App

To run the app:

1.  Right-click the project in Package Explorer and select **Run As > Mule Application**.
2.  After the app deploys, open a web browser.
3.  Enter the following URL to start the OAuth dance: `http://localhost:8081/authorize`.
4.  In the login screen, enter the login information used to access Microsoft Teams and click
    **Login**.
5.  Click **Allow**.
6.  Initiate a flow by entering the associated URL, as shown in the following table. If the URL has
    query parameters, ensure that you include the parameter values:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Flow</th><th>URL</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Create a new team</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/createTeam?displayName={teamDisplayName}&amp;description={teamDescription}&amp;user={teamOwnerUser}</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retrieve the team properties and relationships</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/getTeam?team={createdTeamId}</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retrieve the team channels</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/listChannels</code></p></td><td><p>Returns only the default channel because this example does not create channels.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Onboard a new hire to the team</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/newHireFlow?channelName={channelName}&amp;team={createdTeamId}&amp;userToOnboard={userToBeOnboarded}&amp;channelOwner={channelOwner}</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>For the <code>userToOnboard</code> query parameter, specify a user who is not the channel owner. The JSON response contains the following welcome message:</p></div><div><p><code>"content": "Welcome to the team {channelName}"</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delete a team channel</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/deleteChannel?team={teamId}&amp;channel={channelId}</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Remove a team member</p></td><td><p><code>http://localhost:8081/removeTeamMembers?team={teamId}&amp;member={membershipId}</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## XML for the Examples

Paste this XML code into the **Configuration XML** tab in your project to experiment with the flows
described in the previous sections. When you paste this code, click **Yes** on the **Regenerate
'doc:id' Values** dialog.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:microsoftTeams="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoftTeams"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoftTeams http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoftTeams/current/mule-microsoftTeams.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties"/>
	<microsoftTeams:client-credentials-config name="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config" doc:name="Microsoft Teams Client Credentials Config">
		<microsoftTeams:oauth-client-credentials-connection >
			<microsoftTeams:oauth-client-credentials clientId="${config.oauth.client.credentials.clientId}" clientSecret="${config.oauth.client.credentials.clientSecret}" tokenUrl="${config.oauth.client.credentials.tokenUrl}" scopes="https://graph.microsoft.com/.default" />
		</microsoftTeams:oauth-client-credentials-connection>
	</microsoftTeams:client-credentials-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config name="auth" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8083" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<microsoftTeams:authorization-code-config name="OAuth_Authorization_Code_Config" doc:name="Microsoft Teams Authorization Code Config" >
		<microsoftTeams:oauth-authorization-code-connection >
			<microsoftTeams:oauth-authorization-code consumerKey="${config.oauth.authorization.code.consumerKey}" consumerSecret="${config.oauth.authorization.code.consumerSecret}" authorizationUrl="${config.oauth.authorization.code.authorizationUrl}" accessTokenUrl="${config.oauth.authorization.code.accessTokenUrl}" scopes="https://graph.microsoft.com/.default" />
			<microsoftTeams:oauth-callback-config listenerConfig="auth" callbackPath="/callback" authorizePath="/authorize" externalCallbackUrl="http://localhost:8083/callback" />
		</microsoftTeams:oauth-authorization-code-connection>
	</microsoftTeams:authorization-code-config>
	<flow name="1.CREATE-TEAM" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/createTeam"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	"template@odata.bind": "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/teamsTemplates('standard')",
	description: message.attributes.queryParams.description,
	displayName: message.attributes.queryParams.displayName,
	"members":[
      {
        "@odata.type":"#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember",
        "user@odata.bind":"https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users('" ++ message.attributes.queryParams.user as String ++ "')",
         "roles":[
            "owner"
         ]
      }
   ]
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<microsoftTeams:create-team doc:name="Create team" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="2.GET-CREATED-TEAM">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/getTeam" />
		<microsoftTeams:get-team doc:name="Get team" teamId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.team]" select="#[message.attributes.queryParams.select]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config">
			<microsoftTeams:advanced-query-params >
			</microsoftTeams:advanced-query-params>
		</microsoftTeams:get-team>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="3.LIST-TEAM-MEMBERS-FROM-THE-NEW-TEAM">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" path="/listTeamMembers" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"/>
		<microsoftTeams:list-team-members doc:name="List team members" teamId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.team]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="4.LIST-EXISTING-CHANNELS-FROM-THE-NEW-TEAM">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/listChannels"/>
		<microsoftTeams:list-channels doc:name="List channels" doc:id="e260eb05-4be8-4da9-9cfa-e220ecb4a49a" teamId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.team]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config">
			<microsoftTeams:advanced-query-params >
			</microsoftTeams:advanced-query-params>
		</microsoftTeams:list-channels>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
message]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="5.NEW-HIRE-TEAM-ONBOARDING">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/newHireFlow"/>
		<set-variable value="#[message.attributes.queryParams.channelOwner]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="channelOwner"/>
		<set-variable value="#[message.attributes.queryParams.channelName]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="name"/>
		<set-variable value="#[message.attributes.queryParams.userToOnboard]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="userToOnboard"/>
		<set-variable value="#[message.attributes.queryParams.team]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="team"/>
		<microsoftTeams:add-team-member doc:name="Add team member" teamId="#[vars.team]" userId="#[vars.userToOnboard]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
{
	description: "This channel will be used to onboard new hire " ++ vars.name as String,
	displayName: "Welcome " ++ vars.name as String ++ uuid()[0 to 5],
	membershipType: "private",
	"members":
     [
        {
           "@odata.type":"#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember",
           "user@odata.bind":"https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users('" ++ vars.channelOwner as String ++ "')",
           "roles":["owner"]
        }
     ]
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<microsoftTeams:create-channel doc:name="Create channel" teamId="#[vars.team]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
		<set-variable value="#[payload.id]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="channel"/>
		<microsoftTeams:add-channel-member doc:name="Add channel member" channelId="#[vars.channel]" teamId="#[vars.team]" userId="#[vars.userToOnboard]" owner="true" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	body: {
		content: "Welcome to the team " ++ vars.name as String
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<microsoftTeams:create-message doc:name="Create message" config-ref="OAuth_Authorization_Code_Config" teamId="#[vars.team]" channelId="#[vars.channel]"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
	<flow name="6.DELETE-CHANNEL-FROM-TEAM">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/deleteChannel" />
		<microsoftTeams:delete-channel doc:name="Delete channel" teamId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.team]" channelId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.channel]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
	</flow>
	<flow name="7.REMOVE-TEAM-MEMBER">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/removeTeamMember" />
		<microsoftTeams:remove-team-member doc:name="Remove team member" teamId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.team]" membershipId="#[message.attributes.queryParams.member]" config-ref="OAuth_Client_Credentials_Config"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```
---

# Microsoft Teams Connector 1.1 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Microsoft Teams (Microsoft Teams Connector) gives you access to the Microsoft
Teams APIs.

## Configurations

### Authorization Code Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AuthorizationCodeConfig_OauthAuthorizationCode">OAuth v2.0 Authorization Code</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types that can be provided to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time, in milliseconds, that the consumer will wait for a response before it times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection Types

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the method to use to send the resulting token back to your app. It must be <code>query</code>.</p></td><td><p>query</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration (DEPRECATED)</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumerKey as registered with the service provider</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumerSecret as registered with the service provider</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs authorization endpoint URL</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, this value defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resourceOwnerId that each component should use if it is not otherwise referenced.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a flow to execute immediately after an accessToken is received.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to a <code>&lt;http:listener-config /&gt;</code> to use to create the listener that receives the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the callback endpoint is behind a proxy or must be accessed through a non direct URL, use this parameter to tell the OAuth provider the URL to use to access the callback.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Operations

- [Add Channel Member](#AddChannelMember)
- [Add Group Members](#AddGroupMembers)
- [Add Team Member](#AddTeamMember)
- [Create Channel](#CreateChannel)
- [Create Message](#CreateMessage)
- [Create Team](#CreateTeam)
- [Delete Channel](#DeleteChannel)
- [Delete Team](#DeleteTeam)
- [Get Channel](#GetChannel)
- [Get Team](#GetTeam)
- [List Channel Members](#ListChannelMembers)
- [List Channels](#ListChannels)
- [List Group Members](#ListGroupMembers)
- [List Team Members](#ListTeamMembers)
- [Remove Channel Member](#RemoveChannelMember)
- [Remove Group Member](#RemoveGroupMember)
- [Remove Team Member](#RemoveTeamMember)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)
- [Update Channel](#UpdateChannel)
- [Update Team](#UpdateTeam)

### Client Credentials Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ClientCredentialsConfig_OauthClientCredentials">OAuth v2.0 Client Credentials</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The supported connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element that other components use to reference it.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time, in milliseconds, that the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Connection Types

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Proxy configuration for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration (DEPRECATED)</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, the value defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

## Add Channel Member

`<microsoftTeams:add-channel-member>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id that has a channel with the Id specified in Channel Id parameter</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The channel id where the user should be added</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Corresponding user id to be added as member</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>True if this user should be the owner of the channel.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Add Group Members

`<microsoftTeams:add-group-members>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>GroupMember.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>GroupMember.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Group id where the members should be added</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Member Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of user IDs to add as members in the specified group.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read Timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Add Team Member

`<microsoftTeams:add-team-member>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>TeamMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>TeamMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the user should be added</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User ID of the user to add as a member of the specified team.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Owner</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set to <code>true</code> to designate the user as the owner of the team.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Create Channel

`<microsoftTeams:create-channel>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Channel.Create</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Channel.Create.Group</code>, <code>Channel.Create</code>, <code>Teamwork.Migrate.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the channel should be created</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Information regarding the channel to be created</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Create Message

`<microsoftTeams:create-message>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMessage.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the channel is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel Id where the message should be published</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>The message to be published</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

`<microsoftTeams:delete-channel>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Channel.Delete.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Channel.Delete.All</code>, <code>Channel.Delete.Group</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the channel is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel id to be deleted</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team id to be removed</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the read timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Channel

`<microsoftTeams:get-channel>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Channel.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.Read.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelSettings.Read.Group</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.Group</code>, <code>Channel.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.Read.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the channel is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel Id to be retrieved</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to be retrieved in the response</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response will include information regarding the specified relationship</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the filter query parameter to retrieve just a subset of a collection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN

## Get Team

`<microsoftTeams:get-team>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Team.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>TeamSettings.Read.All</code>, <code>TeamSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>TeamSettings.Read.Group</code>, <code>TeamSettings.ReadWrite.Group</code>, <code>Team.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>TeamSettings.Read.All</code>, <code>TeamSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id to be retrieved</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to be retrieved in the response</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response will include information regarding the specified relationship</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## List Channel Members

`<microsoftTeams:list-channel-members>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMember.Read.All</code>, <code>ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMember.Read.All</code>, <code>ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the channel is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel Id to list its members</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## List Channels

`<microsoftTeams:list-channels>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Channel.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.Read.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelSettings.Read.Group</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.Group</code>, <code>Channel.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.Read.All</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id to list its channels</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to be retrieved in the response</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the filter query parameter to retrieve just a subset of a collection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
