## List Group Members

`<microsoftTeams:list-group-members>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>GroupMember.Read.All</code>, <code>Group.Read.All</code>, <code>GroupMember.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.Read.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>GroupMember.Read.All</code>, <code>Group.Read.All</code>, <code>GroupMember.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.Read.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Group id to list its members</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to be retrieved in the response</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expand</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The response will include information regarding the specified relationship</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the filter query parameter to retrieve just a subset of a collection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order By</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the orderby parameter to specify the sort order of the items returned</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the search parameter to restrict the results of a request to match a search criterion</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Skip</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the skip parameter to set the number of items to skip at the start of a collection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Top</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use the top query parameter to specify the page size of the result set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Retrieves the total count of matching resources.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## List Team Members

`<microsoftTeams:list-team-members>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>TeamMember.Read.All</code>, <code>TeamMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>TeamMember.Read.Group</code>, <code>TeamMember.Read.All</code>, <code>TeamMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id to list its members</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Select</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Properties to be retrieved in the response</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Filter</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Advanced Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN

## Remove Channel Member

`<microsoftTeams:remove-channel-member>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelMember.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team Id where the channel is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel Id that contains the member to be removed</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Membership Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The id of the member to be deleted</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Remove Group Member

`<microsoftTeams:remove-group-member>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>GroupMember.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>GroupMember.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Group.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Group Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Group id where the member to be removed is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Member Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User id that is member in this group</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Unauthorize

`<microsoftTeams:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner id so that itâs impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the resource owner for whom to invalidate access.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

## Update Channel

`<microsoftTeams:update-channel>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All,</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.Group</code>, <code>ChannelSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Team ID where the channel is present</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Channel Id to be updated</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Information regarding the channel to be updated</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

### Throws

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:NOT_FOUND
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:SERVER_ERROR
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:TIMEOUT

## Update Team

`<microsoftTeams:update-team>`

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>TeamSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>TeamSettings.ReadWrite.Group</code>, <code>TeamSettings.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the team to update</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Information to update</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value used by the Read Timeout parameter</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Authorization Code Config](#AuthorizationCodeConfig)
- [Client Credentials Config](#ClientCredentialsConfig)

- MICROSOFTTEAMS:BAD_REQUEST
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:CONNECTIVITY
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:FORBIDDEN
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CONNECTION
- MICROSOFTTEAMS:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

## Types

### Proxy Configuration

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host where the proxy requests will be sent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port where the proxy requests will be sent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The username to authenticate against the proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password to authenticate against the proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tls

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">Crl File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The reconnection strategy to use</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of memory that will be allocated in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded by according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much will be buffer size by expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower will mean that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of instances that will be initially be allowed to be kept in memory in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much will the buffer size by expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower will mean that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Objects</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of instances that will be kept in memory. If more than that is required, then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Microsoft Teams Connector 1.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Microsoft Teams Reference](microsoft-teams-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Microsoft Teams Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `microsoft teams` in the search field.
3.  Click Microsoft Teams in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Microsoft Teams Connector:

- HTTP Listener  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- Scheduler  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP Listener source in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for Microsoft Teams Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Microsoft Teams** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target Microsoft Teams system. Microsoft Teams Connector
supports OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code and OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication methods.

To configure the global element for Microsoft Teams Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the authentication
    method to configure:
    - [OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code](#oauth2-authorization)
    - [OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials](#oauth2-client-credentials-authentication)

      You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
      (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
      properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
      configure them, see
      [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Authentication

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code authentication:

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>User Action</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enter the configuration name.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select <strong>OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code</strong>.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>OAuth consumer key as registered with the Microsoft identity platform.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>OAuth consumer secret that corresponds to the consumer key.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener Config</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>A reference to a <code>&lt;http:listener-config /&gt;</code> to use to create the listener that receives the access token callback endpoint.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback Path</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize Path</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the global element configuration screen to
configure Role authentication:

%header,cols=30s,70a\]

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Field</p></td><td><p>User Action</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><p>Select <strong>OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client ID</p></td><td><p>OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><p>OAuth client secret that corresponds to the client ID.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Microsoft Teams Connector, configure the other required
fields for the connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Microsoft Teams Connector Reference](microsoft-teams-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Microsoft Teams Connector 1.1 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoft-teams
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoft-teams/current/mule4-microsoft-teams.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:$2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/microsoft-teams"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2/current/mule4-microsoft-teams.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
   	<groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
	<artifactId>mule4-microsoft-teams-connector</artifactId>
	<version>1.0.0</version>
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `<version>` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `microsoft teams`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
